title: Application Security and project Management standards
author: Priya
content:
Take Quiz

Ensuring Security of an application at different layer

1.At the user/app Level

  • Use secure authentication, authorization protocols to prevent any unauthorised access to application.
  • Use multi-factor authentication [MFA] to validate user's device and its credentials with OTP verification
  • Use secure access token when accessing another service or API from 1 application using OAuth to authorize user to access your service with a secure secret key.
  • Use SSO[single sign on]feature of SAML to authenticate user's credentials on 1 service with their username and pwd and than authrize that user to access any of its connected service without any need to re-enter its pwd.eg... We signin to company portal using our empid and pwd and than can access any of other services like- sharepoint,outlook,teams,salesforce without any need to re-authenticate ourselves with pwd.By verifying our work or school account at 1 platform ,we can easily use other services of Microsoft .
  • Use SSO to access any social media profile with openId connect that uses gmail based verification to authenticate user in 1 platform. eg...Instagram and than use it to access other platforms like- utube,fb without any need to reauthenticate user with their username and pwd by just SSO of google.

2.At the transport/network layer
Add TLS to our http endpoints which will make our site more secure by encrypting data during transmission to prevent any Eavesdropping or data snooping at the transport layer during transit.Just by adding SSL security to http , data will remain safe and secure with https.


3.At the Data level
At the data level , avoid any data stealing ,session hijacking or malicious code injection into our backend code.Most Common vulnerabilities r -


  • SQL Injection - Use parameterised query using prepared statements , use ORM mapping using JPA & hibernate to access db without any need to write SQL queries explicitly with in-built method of JPARepository/CURDRepository.
  • Cross site Scripting(XSS) - If our code is using Javascript than malicious code injection could be done by attackers.So, to avoid this use escape sequence(/) or encode secret data with hash.Thymeleaf and react uses automatic escaping to secure the data from external injection.
  • Cross Site Request Forgery(CSRF) - If user accessing a site and clicks on certain link or Ad which is not from a trusted source than user will get re-directed to malicious site which will open a backdoor for hackers to steal user's personal information like - user's login credentials , session ID , API Key etc and can use that session info to make call to secure legitimate source and server will process that request.
    Use CSRF Token = Server generates a CSRF Token for each session user is establishing.In out HTML code , we include this token and make its type as hidden.When user submits this html form ,server verifies this token.
    Use custom request header - Some custom headers r added into JS code & that headers r something which attackers can't set to verify its identity at server end.

Some key parameters under security consideration

  1. Do some configuration in the application file as - Never write user credentials ,app/api key or token data in yaml file or any other common config file.Always set such secret info into environment variables so that no attckers could steal any confidential info even if they get access to our backend code.
  2. Never use outdated version of dependencies or libraries that we r using in our code .By using upto date libraries or framework we can keep track of - if there r any new vulnerability came in OWASP .So, by using latest version we can easily trigger or set those flags and headers into our code to prevent it from any unforseabale attacks.
  3. Use standard logging mechanism - Writing logs can easily helps us in spotting any error or bugs and never log any secret info. like- user data,access token, secret key.
  4. Enable audit logging to check info. like number of login attempts.
    Use Actuator that gives production -ready monitoring and management endpoint to do full health checkup of app.It gives all metric details such as - http request count ,failure,unauthorised access .Show request code 400,500,501,200 for request success or failure.
    - Checks audit events such as login attempt, failure,access denial with audit logging.
    - Give application health input that all dependencies r upto date,app servers r up.
    - Display environment properties in which user currently working.Check if any sensitive info. r not exposed within that environment.
    So, with actuators , developers get a visibility into authentication events , request pattern and frequency of request call for specific service to detect potential risk at an early stage.

Some other key things should be checked -

  • During any REST API call,use JWT tokens instead of using cookies to send user data with each request to server
  • In session management from within app,use secure cookies flag (As secure,httpOnly) and make sessions short-lived.Session management ensures user's interaction with the system/service to be remain private.consistent and authenticated throughout the session.

How Session Management works Internally

When user log into an app, server creates a session with the user and log certain info like - user's login credentials ,user's preference on the type of data they r accessing and create a unique session Id which is stored in cookies or access token.And this sessionId is sent with request that user make with server.

  1. Now since with each session , secure cookies r used which prevents attackers from stealing session id.
  2. On new login , server generates new session id.So, that old session id which if accessed maliciously by attacker cannot get access to the application with that old session id as it became inactive to be reused for any future login.
  3. Kept session short and make users logout of the app if time exceeds inorder to keep data safe and secure from intruder to peep into your cache/history to gain access to your private code

Hence , security is made for the app by -
- Limiting session lifetime.
- Store session at the server side to avoid client side tampering.
- Use secure cookies to prevent XSS/CSRF attacks.

Ensuring proper code building and review

Follow agile/DevOps practice in your daily workflow.

  • Schedule daily stand up calls
  • Do sprint planning
  • Code reviews
  • Sprint retrospection

Check code quality to see whether code is maintainable ,having low code complexity , check for bugs and errors, check proper code standard r maintained with updated version of software and libraries.
- Test coverage of code should be maximum (75 - 90%)
- Code pipeline building - It will ensure every commit must trigger CI build.
- SonarQube check before PR.
- Code Review - Before merging , PR review should be given to developers.And no PR approval if code build pipeline is failing.
- For continuous deployment - Some manual testing should be done before releasing code in production env.

Working with Sonarqube

It is a software testing tool [static testing tool] which is used to test & improve the quality of the code & help in fixing the error.If the code is written in a much complex way Sonar will suggest to write it in a simpler way.If finds security error ie... It helps to make the code more secure when we r deploying it in any environment.We can configure it with Jenkins, so that everytime we don't have to manually involve in sonar testing of our code .Jenkins will take care to test our code automatically.Instead of Jenkins we can also use default pipeline building tool if we r working with other online code testing and deployment platform like of Azure DevOps which has inbuilt support to automate CI build.If working on GitHub we can use github actions , in gitlab with gitlab CI.
SonarQube can track bugs in our code or can raise issue in a piece of code that it feels faulty.It gives us a branch level analysis ie... It not only scan the master branch but also checks the other branches introducing error.

SonarLint is a SonarQube plugin which helps to easily integrate SonarQube with any IDE without any need to download & install the whole Sonar software. It offers reports on duplicate code,unit test,code complexity,code coverage, comments, bugs,any security vulnerability.It can track all the repositories at a common place & a profile[which r like some specific rule or coding standard to be followed] is set in sonar.Based on that profile our code quality is monitored. & vulnerabilities r identified.

Code coverage using SonarQube
Code coverage gives us the metric of the percentage of production code that is tested. either unit testing or integration testing.If the code coverage is good than we can have the faster release cycle.
Everytime we will try to build & run our code in azure repo .it will do unit testing, code coverage testing and all other testing & pre production steps involved

CORS ensuring Application Security

Cross origin resource sharing allows browser to block access to any external api or web service in an another site for security reasons. Any external API we r using in our app r either accessed through fetch() or XMLHttpResponse which follow same origin policy.
Making external api access using fetch()

const fetchPromise = fetch("https://bar.other");
fetchPromise .then((response) => response.json()) .then((data) => { console.log(data); });


We write -> Access-Control-Allow-Origin : https://bar.other
to allow cors using this site accessing our site in browser. Server respond with Access-Control-Allow-Origin : https://bar.other to allow origin of this domain to read our site information .
Now cors policy clearly states that a site can only access resources coming from the. same origin.Accessing any external service or API will be a cross origin resource and such resource can only be accessed using some specific http headers that allows cors to make easier access of those resources by our app with no blocking calls .
To allow cross origin responses , we use right CORS headers called http headers.By using such headers it let server to allow which origin can access data from our site.
Cors https response headers -
Cors mechanism ensures secure cross origin access and data transfer between server and browser through http headers.

  • Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true if a request is made for a resource with credentials, than the response to the request can be exposed when the credentials flag is true.
  • Access-Control-Allow-Origin : https://bar.other

What r the ways through which we can allow any external API read data through our app which is blocked by CORS

  1. Create a backend proxy server app.get('/proxy', async (req, res) => { const response = await fetch( 'https://target-site.com/api' ); const data = await response.json(); res.json(data); }); Than use - fetch('/proxy') Instead of directly accessing fetch('https://target-site.com/api')
  2. Use serverless function - like through Google script or vercel
  3. àdd cors header Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * Or Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://yourdomain.com
  4. use browser extension to locally disable cors check in your browser.
  5. Use webhook - external service or API will respond to the events triggered after changes to our app.
  6. With JSONP - If CORS block access to
    fetch( 'https://interviewprepforinsiders.blogspot.com/feeds/posts/default?alt=json' )
    than use this instead
    script src=" https://interviewprepforinsiders.blogspot.com/feeds/posts/default ?alt=json-in-script &callback=handleFeed ">/script> and define -
    function handleFeed(data){ console.log(data); }

How enabling cors header to access external api cause csrf attack

CSRF Attacks - This is 1 of known OWASP vulnerability attcking trusted sites by stealing confidential user data such as - their credentials,api key or other session information with the help of a malicious site.Such that attacker can steal secret information of our site through browser or any open active session of our site and can make genuine request to our server using our details.

Such attacks r made to steal real information of users like of bank details , any secret api details by stealing our secure tokens or pwd and they mainly attack sites with JS code .Many banking sites now tried to use secure cookies , and keep users' sessions short to prevent such attacks.
These attacks r triggered by users click on browser from a trusted site to an unknown site that may look informational at once butactually opens a secret door by our browser to steal users secret data.

- cors allows preventing csrf attack as most sites used cookies. And if suppose we r accessing any site than browser store our site information ,user credentials in the form of cookies. And now when user access any malicious site than it can steal cookies information from browser but cors will inhibit browser to give info of 1 site to another .This way cors will block malicious site to access info of other site or even cookies info. But by setting http response header as - Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
This wildcard * allowing every other site to read data from our site . It will violate security rules set by cors by allowing every every site to access origin of every other sites. And it increases security risk of csrf .
